content:
You're probably reading this page because you've attempted to
access some part of my blog (Wandering
Thoughts) or CSpace, the wiki thing it's
part of. Unfortunately you're using a browser (or client library)
that my anti-crawler precautions consider suspicious because it's
sending a Accept
HTTP header with a suspicious value, such as "*/*", while claiming
to be a browser. Actual browsers are more selective in their HTTP
Accept header values.

Due to the ongoing problems with abusive high volume crawlers
using forged User-Agent values (apparently in part to gather data
for LLM training), this combination of browser User-Agent and Accept
header value is not accepted.

If this is in error and you're using a current version of your
browser of choice, you can contact me at my current place at the
university (you should be able to work out the email address
from that). If possible, please let me know what browser you're
using and so on, ideally with its exact User-Agent string. Please
note that I'm unlikely to make an exemption for software that is
actively impersonating Chrome or Firefox in its User-Agent, especially
if its User-Agent is essentially indistinguishable from a legitimate
browser User-Agent string. Crawling software that isn't prepared to
admit it is being anti-social, and I am no longer willing to help
anti-social software.

Chris Siebenmann, 2025-12-17
